title: 30-Minute Chicken Parm Meatballs

ingredients.item: panko breadcrumbs, plus more for rolling
Parmesan cheese, grated, plus more for garnish
Italian seasoning (or 1 tsp each dried thyme, oregano, and parsley)
fresh basil, finely chopped, plus more for garnish
garlic cloves, grated (or 1/2 tsp garlic powder)
French bread, sourdough, cooked pasta, or cauliflower rice

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Combine ground chicken, breadcrumbs, Parmesan, Italian seasoning, salt, basil, garlic, and egg in a large bowl. Beat the egg with a fork first, then use your hands to mix everything together.

Spray a 10-inch oven-safe skillet with cooking spray. Pour about 1/3 cup panko into a shallow bowl. Form 10 meatballs, roll each in the panko to coat, and place in the skillet. Spray the tops with cooking spray.
Bake for 15 minutes. Remove from oven and carefully pour the marinara sauce around the meatballs — the hot skillet may cause some splatter.
Scatter mozzarella over the top and return to the oven for 10 minutes, until the cheese is melted.
Garnish with fresh basil and Parmesan. Serve immediately from the skillet over bread, pasta, or cauliflower rice.

title: 30-Minute Crunchy Cheesy Bean Bake

ingredients.item: cooked white beans (gigantes or cannellini), or 2 (15-oz) cans, rinsed and drained

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.

Heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a large deep oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Add onion, garlic, red pepper flakes, a large pinch of salt, and a few grinds of pepper. Cook until the onion is soft, about 3 minutes.

Add tomato paste and cook, smashing it with a wooden spoon, until it turns a deeper colour, about 1 minute. Stir in diced tomatoes, oregano, and water. Increase heat to medium-high and cook for 5 minutes until warmed through. Add the beans and cook, stirring occasionally, for 4–5 minutes until heated through.
Mix panko, Parmesan, thyme leaves, and remaining 3 tablespoons olive oil in a small bowl until every crumb is coated in oil.
Taste the bean mixture and adjust salt as needed — add a small pinch at a time until the flavours pop.
Arrange mozzarella slices over the beans, then scatter the panko mixture evenly around the slices. Bake for 15 minutes until bubbly and golden. Broil briefly at the end if more browning is needed.
Serve with a green salad and/or crusty bread.

title: 30-Minute Pork and Cabbage Noods

ingredients.item: green cabbage (half a head), core removed, thinly sliced (or 10-oz bag shredded cabbage)
fresh ginger, grated (or 1/2 tsp ground ginger)
toasted sesame oil, plus more for serving
hot sauce or chili paste (sriracha, gochujang, sambal oelek — optional)
toasted sesame seeds, plus more for serving

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti per package instructions. Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining. Drain and shake dry.
Meanwhile, slice the cabbage as thinly as possible. Grate the ginger and garlic. Slice the chives.

In a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat, press ground pork into a thin layer. Cook undisturbed for 5–7 minutes until browned underneath. Break into small crumbles, then add cabbage, ginger, garlic, and a pinch of salt. Cook until cabbage is wilted, 2–4 minutes. Remove from heat until the noodles are ready.

Add cooked spaghetti, 1/2 cup pasta water, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil to the skillet. Add hot sauce if using. Set over medium heat and toss for 1–2 minutes until the pasta is coated in sauce. Add more pasta water as needed until the noodles are glossy and saucy.
Remove from heat and stir in chives and sesame seeds. Serve with an extra drizzle of sesame oil and more sesame seeds.

title: Autumn Chop

ingredients.item: butternut squash, cubed into 1-inch pieces (fresh or frozen)
curly kale, de-ribbed and chopped (or 10–12 oz bag pre-chopped)
dates, chopped (or 1/4 cup dried cranberries or golden raisins)
candied or roasted pecans or pistachios

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Make the dressing: combine all dressing ingredients in a jar and shake until combined (or whisk in a bowl). Set aside.
Toss red onion, butternut squash, and herb sprigs with 2 tablespoons of the dressing and 1 teaspoon kosher salt on a rimmed sheet pan. Spread evenly and roast for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, place chopped kale in a large serving bowl. Add 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt and massage with your hands 5–10 times until softened but not wet. Add the apple, dates, nuts, and cheese.

After 20 minutes, stir the vegetables. Add the chicken breasts to the pan, drizzle with a little dressing, and season generously with salt and pepper. Roast for 15–20 minutes until chicken reaches 160°F internally (it will carry over to 165°F as it rests) and squash is completely tender — you should be able to easily squish a piece with a fork. If the chicken is done before the squash, remove it and continue roasting the squash.
Let the chicken cool enough to handle, then chop into bite-sized pieces. Discard the herb sprigs. Add the chicken, squash, and onions to the salad bowl.
Add dressing a little at a time and toss to coat — you can always add more but can't take it off. Season to taste with salt and pepper and serve.
notes: Frozen butternut squash works — cook straight from frozen.

title: Beef Enchilada Casserole
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.

Warm a 12-inch ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat. Add olive oil, onion, bell pepper, garlic, ground beef, cauliflower rice, taco seasoning, and salt. Cook 5–6 minutes, breaking beef into small crumbles, until beef is cooked through and vegetables are tender.

Taste and adjust seasoning. Stir in flour until fully absorbed, then stir in diced tomatoes and chopped greens. Cook until greens wilt and mixture thickens. (If there's excess liquid, increase heat and stir until evaporated.) Turn off heat.
Char tortillas directly over a gas flame, or toast in a dry skillet over medium-high heat.
Press tortillas into the beef mixture, layering imperfectly with some filling between each. Top with grated cheese.
Bake 10–12 minutes until cheese is browned and mixture is bubbling. Let cool slightly before scooping onto plates.
Serve with sour cream, pickled red onions, salsa, hot sauce, lime wedges, and/or cilantro.

title: Boudreaux Gumbo
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: boneless, skinless chicken thighs (raw), trimmed and halved
low-sodium chicken stock (or water + Better Than Bouillon)
fresh okra, sliced ¼-inch thick (or frozen pre-sliced)

steps: Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Whisk in flour and reduce heat to medium-low. Cook the roux, stirring every 1–2 minutes, for about 30 minutes until it reaches a rich peanut-butter brown. (For deeper flavor, continue 10–15 more minutes until milk-chocolate brown. Do not let it burn.)
While the roux cooks, dice onion, celery, and bell pepper; mince garlic; trim and halve chicken thighs; slice okra and sausage.
Once the roux is dark brown, stir in onion, celery, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook 5 minutes, stirring. Stir in Cajun seasoning, smoked paprika, and tomato paste and cook 1 more minute.
Stir in stock and tomato sauce. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium. Add chicken thighs and bay leaves and cook uncovered for 15 minutes.
Add okra and sausage and cook 15 more minutes until okra is tender. (The gumbo will look slimy shortly after the okra goes in — this is normal and will resolve.)
Remove chicken thighs and shred with two forks. Stir back in. Discard bay leaves.
Taste and season with salt and pepper in small increments until the flavors pop.
Serve over white rice with hot sauce (Crystal preferred) and sliced scallions.
notes: Serves 6–8 — good leftover opportunity for a household of 3. Serve over rice.

title: Butternut Squash Farro-tto
steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.

Warm olive oil in an ovenproof pot (ideally a cast iron Dutch oven) over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook, stirring frequently, 3–4 minutes until soft. Add farro, garlic, and oregano and cook 1 more minute.
Stir in broth, butternut squash, cauliflower rice, 1 teaspoon salt, and garlic powder. Bring to a boil, then turn off the heat.
Cover and transfer to the oven. Bake 30–35 minutes until farro is tender and most liquid is absorbed.
Smash the squash slightly with the back of a spoon. Stir in spinach, lemon juice, Parmesan, Gruyère, frozen broccoli, and pepper until spinach is wilted and the farro-tto is creamy.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Serve with extra Parmesan.
notes: Peas substituted with frozen broccoli.

title: Caramelized Onion, Steak, and Cheese Skillet
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: large yellow onion, thinly sliced into half-moons
green bell peppers, cored, seeded, and thinly sliced
tapioca flour or arrowroot starch (can sub cornstarch)
coconut aminos (or reduced-sodium tamari or soy sauce)

steps: Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell peppers and cook, stirring occasionally, until onion is caramelized and peppers are soft, 25–30 minutes. (Reduce heat to medium-low if browning too quickly; add a splash of water if needed.)
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add mushrooms and garlic and cook, stirring occasionally, until mushrooms are softened, 5–7 minutes.
Create a well in the center of the pan and add remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil. Add shaved steak and cook, stirring frequently, until cooked through and browned, about 2 minutes.

Add tapioca flour, salt, and pepper and mix until well combined. Stir in coconut aminos and reduce heat to low. Sprinkle mozzarella over the top, cover (use a sheet pan if the skillet has no lid), and cook until sauce is thickened and cheese is melted, about 5 minutes.
Divide among plates and serve.
notes: Deconstructed Philly cheesesteak. Avocado oil subbed with olive oil. Serve straight from the skillet or in a hoagie roll.

title: Chicken Meatballs with Lemon Orzo

ingredients.item: lemon (preferably Meyer), zested and juiced, divided

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Grease a 12-inch oven-proof skillet with olive oil.

Whisk the egg in a large bowl. Add ground chicken, 1/2 cup Parmesan, rosemary (mince very finely — large pieces are unpleasant in a bite), breadcrumbs, lemon zest, 1 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes. Mix with a fork or hands until just combined.
Grease a tablespoon measure with oil. Scoop heaping spoonfuls (about 2 tablespoons each) into the skillet — you should get 14–18 meatballs. Grease your hands and roll into balls.
Bake for 18 minutes. Meanwhile, mince the shallot and garlic, keeping them separate. Halve the zested lemon.
Transfer cooked meatballs to a plate. Place the skillet over medium heat and melt butter. Place the lemon halves cut-side down in the skillet to char — this deepens the flavour.

Add shallot and a pinch of salt to the other side of the skillet and cook for 5–7 minutes until tender. Stir in garlic, orzo, and remaining 1/4 teaspoon red pepper flakes and cook for 1 minute. Remove the lemon halves. Stir in 2 cups stock and bring to a boil. Reduce to medium-low and cook, stirring often, for 8–10 minutes until orzo is al dente.
Stir in more stock as needed to keep it loose and saucy. Add the frozen broccoli, remaining 1/2 cup Parmesan, and juice from the charred lemon.
Turn off the heat, nestle the meatballs on top, and cover for a few minutes to heat through. Garnish with Parmesan and red pepper flakes.
notes: Peas substituted with frozen broccoli.

title: Chicken, Mushroom, and Farro Soup
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: large leek, halved vertically and thinly sliced
kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
low-sodium chicken stock (or water + Better Than Bouillon)

ingredients.amount: grated Parmesan ⅓ cup, cream cheese 4 oz, heavy cream ½ cup, half and half ⅓ cup, or plain yogurt ⅓ cup

steps: Add carrots, celery, leek, mushrooms, and garlic to a large cold pot. Drizzle with olive oil, add 1 teaspoon salt and several grinds of pepper, and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, 7–10 minutes until vegetables are tender and liquid has evaporated. (If still liquidy once tender, increase to high heat and stir constantly until evaporated.)
Add chicken thighs, farro, rosemary sprigs, kale, and stock. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low, cover with lid slightly ajar, and cook 20 minutes.

Remove herb sprigs. Transfer chicken to a cutting board and shred with two forks. (If not shredding easily or not at 165°F, return to pot and cook a few minutes more.) Return shredded chicken to the pot.
Stir in juice of ½ lemon to start, adjusting up to the full lemon to taste. Season with salt and pepper.
Optional — to make creamy: stir in your chosen creamy addition and simmer a few minutes until incorporated and thickened. (It may look curdled at first — keep stirring.) Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve topped with grated Parmesan, croutons, or a drizzle of chili crisp.
notes: Serves 6–8 — great leftover opportunity for a household of 3. Optional creamy additions listed above.

title: Creamy Cajun Turkey, Sausage, and Wild Rice Soup
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: mild or spicy ground Italian sausage (links or bulk)
low-sodium chicken stock (or water + Better Than Bouillon)
ingredients.amount: 6 cups, plus up to 2 more if needed

ingredients.item: cooked turkey or chicken, shredded or chopped
cream cheese, cut into small pieces (or ⅓ cup grated Parm)
toasted sourdough with butter and flaky salt (optional but recommended)

steps: Cook sausage in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking into small pieces, until crispy and browned. Transfer to a bowl with a slotted spoon, leaving the grease in the pot.
Add butter to the pot. Once melted, add celery, carrots, onion, and mushrooms. Cook, stirring every minute, 10–15 minutes until all moisture has evaporated.
Stir in flour, salt, pepper, rosemary, smoked paprika, and Cajun seasoning. Cook 1 minute.
Add stock, kale, wild rice, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low, cover, and simmer 30–40 minutes until rice is tender.
Remove bay leaves. Stir in shredded turkey or chicken, cooked sausage, cream cheese (or Parm), and juice of ½ lemon. Simmer uncovered a few minutes until cream cheese has melted in.
Taste and adjust salt and lemon juice. Add up to 2 more cups stock if a thinner consistency is preferred.
Serve with toasted sourdough, good butter, and flaky salt.
notes: Serves 6–8 — leftover opportunity for a household of 3. Creamy or brothy depending on whether you add cream cheese/Parm.

title: Creamy Chicken, Leek, and Sun-Dried Tomato Orzotto
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
large leek, thinly sliced and washed
julienned sun-dried tomatoes (oil-packed jar or dry-packed bag)
grated Parmesan, plus more for garnish

steps: Trim fat from chicken breasts and cut into 1-inch cubes. Season with oregano, 1 1/4 teaspoons salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, garlic powder, and red pepper flakes if using. Toss to coat on the cutting board.
Thinly slice and wash the leek. Mince the garlic.

Warm olive oil in a wide skillet or braiser over medium-high heat. Add chicken in an even layer and cook until browned on one side, 2–3 minutes. Add butter, leek, and garlic and stir for 2 minutes until the leek is tender. Move on if the garlic starts to darken before 2 minutes.

Stir in chicken stock, orzo, and sun-dried tomatoes (leave the oil behind if using oil-packed). Bring to a boil, then reduce to medium-low. Stir in the heavy cream and simmer for 10–12 minutes, stirring every minute, until the orzo is cooked and the sauce has thickened.

Turn off the heat. Tear in the spinach and grate in the lemon zest. Add the Parmesan and stir until the spinach is wilted, 1–2 minutes. If the pasta has tightened up, loosen with a splash of stock or water.
Taste and adjust with salt, red pepper flakes, or lemon juice. Serve in bowls garnished with more Parmesan and black pepper.
notes: Under 30 minutes. Big batch — serves ~6, good leftover opportunity for a household of 3.

title: Creamy Corn Pasta

ingredients.item: farfalle, orecchiette, rigatoni, or cheese tortellini
fresh corn ears, kernels removed (about 2 3/4 cups) OR frozen white corn
red pepper flakes, plus more to taste
Parmesan cheese, grated, plus more for garnish

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook pasta until al dente per package instructions. Before draining, reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water. Drain.
Meanwhile, dice the shallot, mince the garlic, and cut the kernels off the corn — lay ears flat on the board to keep kernels from flying.

Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add shallot, garlic, and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring frequently, for 3–5 minutes until the shallot is translucent. Add corn and 1/2 cup water and cook over medium-high for 4–5 minutes until tender. For frozen corn, add straight from the bag — no thawing needed, may take an extra minute or two.

Transfer corn mixture to a blender. Add Parmesan, juice of 1 lemon, and 1 teaspoon salt. Blend on high until smooth, adding reserved pasta water as needed to make it pourable. Taste and adjust with more lemon, salt, or a pinch of sugar if the corn isn't very sweet.
Thinly slice the basil leaves.
Return drained pasta to the pot. Pour in the corn sauce and a big handful of basil. Toss to combine, adding more pasta water if too thick. Adjust seasoning with lemon, salt, or red pepper flakes.
Serve garnished with more basil, Parmesan, and red pepper flakes.
notes: If you forget to reserve pasta water, see the source link for a workaround.

title: Crispy Chicken Chili Verde Street Tacos

ingredients.item: cheddar, pepper jack, or Monterey Jack, shredded
street taco tortillas, flour or corn
mayo, sour cream, or Greek yogurt (or a mix)

steps: Position an oven rack in the top third of the oven and preheat to 425°F. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment.
Combine ground chicken, corn, green chiles, 2 teaspoons taco seasoning, 1 teaspoon salt, and shredded cheese in a large bowl.
If using corn tortillas, wrap the stack in a damp paper towel and microwave for 30 seconds until pliable. Skip this step for flour tortillas.

Scoop a spoonful of the raw meat mixture onto half of each tortilla. Fold closed and press. Optionally sprinkle extra cheese on the meat before folding for cheesier tacos. Don't worry if corn tortillas crack slightly — the result will be the same.
Arrange tacos on the prepared baking sheets at least 1 inch apart. Spray all over with cooking spray, flip, and spray the second side.
Bake for 15–20 minutes until golden-brown and crispy.
Make the sauce: stir together the creamy base, remaining 1 teaspoon taco seasoning, juice of half the lime, and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
Let tacos cool a few minutes before serving with the sauce.
notes: Check the source link for shopping notes on ground chicken and sodium-free taco seasoning.

title: Crispy Farro with Sausage and Asparagus

ingredients.item: mild or hot Italian sausage, casings removed
scallions (1 bunch), trimmed and thinly sliced on a diagonal, dark green parts kept separate
asparagus (1 bunch), trimmed and thinly sliced on a diagonal into 1/2-inch pieces

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add farro and simmer for 15–20 minutes until al dente — tender but still slightly chewy. Drain, rinse under cold water, and shake thoroughly dry.
While farro cooks, remove sausage casings, slice scallions (keeping dark green tops separate), and slice asparagus.

Wipe out the pot (or use a large skillet). Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high. Add sausage and cook, breaking into small pieces, for 8–10 minutes until crispy. Transfer to a plate with a slotted spoon, leaving the fat in the pot.

Add farro and scallion whites to the sausage fat. Spread flat and cook undisturbed until golden on the bottom, 3–5 minutes — listen for the sizzle to turn to a crackle. Add a splash of olive oil if anything starts to burn.
Add asparagus and a big pinch of salt. Stir, spread flat again, and cook until the bottom is golden once more, 3–5 minutes.
Return the sausage and any collected juices to the pot. Stir for 1–2 minutes until the asparagus is crisp-tender. Turn off the heat and stir in the scallion greens and red wine vinegar.
Season to taste with salt and more vinegar, adding a small splash at a time, until punchy and well-balanced.
notes: If you can't find pearled farro, cooking time will be longer — see source link for guidance.

title: Curry Roasted Chicken and Cauliflower Salad

ingredients.item: cauliflower head, cut into 1-inch flat florets
small honeycrisp apple, cut into batons
dates, pitted and chopped (or 1/3 cup dried cranberries or golden raisins)
Parmesan, shaved with a vegetable peeler
roasted salted pistachios, sunflower seeds, pepitas, or almonds

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Cut cauliflower into 1-inch pieces and thinly slice the onion.

Toss cauliflower, onion, and chicken thighs on a large rimmed baking sheet with olive oil, 2 teaspoons salt, pepper, paprika, 1 teaspoon curry powder, coriander, and garlic powder until evenly coated.

Roast for 30 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F and cauliflower is tender and golden. If the chicken is done but the cauliflower needs more colour, remove the chicken and broil the cauliflower on high for a few minutes, watching constantly.
Meanwhile, make the dressing: shake or stir together mayo, Greek yogurt, lime juice, water, honey, remaining 1 teaspoon curry powder, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and pepper until combined. Taste and adjust.
Cut apple into batons. Pit and chop dates.

Place arugula in a large shallow serving bowl and season with a pinch of salt. Chop the chicken and arrange on top with the roasted cauliflower and onions, shaved Parmesan, dates, and nuts. Dress to taste, starting with half the dressing and adding more as needed.

title: Fall Chicken and Kale Salad

ingredients.item: garlic powder (or 1 small garlic clove, grated)
honey or maple syrup (optional, if dressing is too tangy)
curly or lacinato kale, tough stems removed, finely chopped
cooked chicken breast, finely chopped (rotisserie works well)
honeycrisp apple, cut into thin batons
dates, pitted and finely chopped (or 1/4 cup raisins, dried cranberries, or dried cherries)

steps: Whisk olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, several grinds of pepper, and garlic powder in a large serving bowl. Taste and add honey or maple syrup if too tangy.

Add the chopped kale and a big pinch of salt. Massage the dressing into the kale until it softens — about 7 squeezes between your palms. Don't worry if it looks overdressed; the chicken will absorb the excess.
Add the chicken, apple, almonds, cheddar, and dates. Toss to coat. Let sit for 10 minutes if time allows before serving.
notes: Rotisserie chicken makes this weeknight-fast. Serves 3 exactly — no leftovers for a household of 3.

title: Good Mood Chicken and Bean Soup
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: boneless, skinless chicken (breasts, tenders, or thighs)
low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth (or water + Better Than Bouillon)
grated Parmesan, plus more for serving

steps: Add beans, kale, garlic, chicken, red pepper flakes, sun-dried tomatoes, broth, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper to a large heavy-bottomed pot. (If you have a Parm rind, add it now for extra flavor — remove before serving.) If the kale isn't fully submerged, it will wilt down quickly; add more broth if the chicken and beans are still not covered.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low and cook uncovered for 20 minutes.
Shred the chicken directly in the pot using two forks. Lightly smash some of the beans with the back of a spoon.
Stir in heavy cream and Parmesan until well combined. Taste and adjust salt as needed.
Serve topped with a generous layer of grated Parmesan.
notes: 40 min, mostly hands-off. Serves 4–6 — leftover opportunity for a household of 3.

title: Good Soup
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: large yellow onion, peeled and quartered
kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
large bunch collards or lacinato kale
dried bay leaves or fresh rosemary sprigs

steps: Pulse carrots, fennel, onion, and garlic in a food processor until very finely chopped. (Alternatively, finely chop by hand or blend one vegetable at a time in a blender.)

Cook sausage in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat, breaking into ½-inch pieces, until golden-brown all over, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate with a slotted spoon, leaving the fat in the pot.

Add 3 tablespoons olive oil to the pot and stir in the chopped vegetables. Season with about 1 teaspoon salt and ½ teaspoon pepper. Cook stirring often for 5 minutes, then partially cover and reduce to medium-low for 15 more minutes. (Vegetables should soften but not brown — lower heat if needed.) Meanwhile, roughly chop the collards.
Stir in harissa, tomato paste, cumin, and lemon zest. Cook 1–2 minutes until the paste darkens to a burnt-red colour.
Add water, Better Than Bouillon, lentils, barley, collards, Parm rind, bay leaves, and 1½ teaspoons salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low, cover fully, and cook for 45 minutes.

While the soup cooks, make croutons: coat the bottom of a large skillet generously with olive oil over medium-high heat. Add bread cubes and toss to coat, adding more oil as needed. Spread in an even layer, season with salt and garlic powder. Toast undisturbed until golden on the first side, 3–5 minutes, then stir and toast until golden all over. Set aside to cool.
Stir cooked sausage and lemon juice into the soup and heat through, about 3 minutes. Remove and discard the Parm rind and bay leaves.
Ladle into bowls and top with croutons and a generous amount of grated Parmigiano-Reggiano.
notes: 90 min total, ~20 min active. Serves 6–8 — great leftover opportunity for a household of 3.

title: Green Chicken Tortilla Soup

ingredients.item: white beans (cannellini, navy, great Northern, or chickpeas), drained but not rinsed
cilantro (leaves and tender stems), finely chopped
shredded cheddar or pepper jack cheese

steps: (Optional tortilla strips) Preheat oven to 375°F. Toss tortilla slices with oil, salt, chili powder, and garlic powder on a rimmed baking sheet until evenly coated. Bake for 15–20 minutes until crispy and browned.

Add chicken stock, salsa verde, green chiles (not drained), white beans, chicken thighs, zucchini, cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika to a large pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook for 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken and shred with two forks. Return to the pot with the frozen corn, kale, and cilantro. Cook until the kale is wilted.
Taste and adjust salt as needed (saltiness varies depending on stock and beans).
Serve topped with tortilla strips or chips, sour cream, cheese, and more cilantro.
notes: Shortcut: use pre-cooked chicken or turkey — skip the 30-minute simmer and just heat through.

title: Happy Bowls

ingredients.amount: 1 can cannellini beans + 1 tbsp olive oil): 2 cups

ingredients.item: fresh soft green herbs (basil, cilantro, or dill)
hot water, plus more as needed
small garlic clove (or 1/8 tsp garlic powder)
head of cauliflower, cut into thin florets
garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika
cooked grain (farro, rice, or similar — about 1 to 1 1/2 cups dry)
fresh spinach or other greens, chopped

ingredients.amount: crumbled feta, hard-boiled eggs, pickled red onions, roasted nuts or seeds, extra fresh herbs

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line two baking sheets with parchment.
Add cashews to a blender and cover with hot water. Set aside to soak while you prep the veg.
Scrub and cube the sweet potato and beet into 1-inch pieces. Place on separate halves of one baking sheet.

Cut the cauliflower into thin florets: halve through the stem, lay cut-side down, and slice into 1/2-inch planks, then cut apart florets. Place on one side of the second baking sheet. Add the sliced onion to the other side.

Drizzle all veg with olive oil and season each section with a big pinch of salt, a pinch of pepper, and shakes of garlic powder, onion powder, and smoked paprika. Toss each section to coat, keeping them separate.
Roast for 30–40 minutes until golden and fork-tender. Beets will be firmer than sweet potatoes — that's fine.
Meanwhile, cook your grains per package instructions and wash and chop your greens.

Make the cashew sauce: drain the soaking water from the blender. Add herbs, apple cider vinegar, 1/2 cup hot water, garlic, and salt. Blend until completely smooth, adding more hot water 1 tablespoon at a time until the sauce is thick but drizzleable. Taste and adjust salt.
Assemble bowls: add grains and greens to the base, top with roasted veg, drizzle generously with cashew sauce, and finish with any toppings.
notes: Cashew allergy sub: replace cashews with 1 can cannellini beans + 1 tbsp olive oil in the sauce.

title: Hella Green Pasta

ingredients.item: garlic cloves, peeled and roughly chopped (buy pre-peeled)
spinach (about 12 oz), ends trimmed (or 2 bags baby spinach)
pasta (bucatini or any long thick noodle)

steps: Cut the rind off the Parmesan, cut into chunks, and blend until finely grated. Leave in the blender for the sauce.
Add garlic and olive oil to a small pot. Cook over low heat for 5–7 minutes until garlic is light golden and softened. Remove from heat and let cool.
Bring a large pot of heavily salted water (about 2 tablespoons salt) to a boil. Add kale and cook for 2 minutes. Add spinach and cook for 2–3 more minutes until both are wilted. Remove from heat.
Use tongs to transfer the greens to the blender, squeezing out excess water as you go — a little water is fine. Do NOT drain the pot. Return the pot to high heat and bring back to a boil.
Cook pasta per package instructions. Reserve 1 cup of cooking water, then drain the pasta and return it to the pot.

Make the sauce: add the cooled garlic and all its oil to the blender with the zest and juice of 1 lemon and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt. Blend on high until smooth. Add reserved pasta water a little at a time until the sauce is the consistency of a pourable marinara — thick but drizzleable. Taste and adjust with more salt or lemon juice.
Pour 1 cup of sauce into the pasta and toss. Add more sauce until the pasta is glossy and well-coated — about 1 1/2 cups total. Stir in more pasta water if needed to thin it out.
Serve garnished with more Parmesan and red pepper flakes.
notes: Save the Parmesan rind — it makes a great addition to brothy soups like Lemony Chicken Orzo.

title: 30-Minute Honey Gochujang Glazed Salmon

ingredients.item: salmon filets (6-oz each), cut into 1-inch cubes

steps: Combine rice, coconut milk, 1 1/4 cups water, 2 tablespoons honey, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a wide saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce to the lowest heat possible. Cover and cook for 10 minutes.

Poke holes all over a large sheet of parchment paper. After 10 minutes, stir the rice, lay the parchment on top, and add the broccoli florets on top of the parchment. Cover and cook on low for 10 more minutes until broccoli is tender and rice is cooked through. Keep covered until serving.
While the rice cooks, whisk together soy sauce, remaining 3 tablespoons honey, fish sauce, gochujang, 1 tablespoon water, and garlic powder in a bowl.

Season salmon cubes with a small pinch of salt. Warm enough oil to coat a large skillet (preferably non-stick) over medium-high heat. Add salmon in an even layer and cook for 3–4 minutes until crisp on the bottom. Use a thin spatula to flip without tearing, then cook for another 3 minutes until the salmon releases easily.

Add the sauce and butter to the skillet. Increase heat to high and cook for 2–3 minutes, gently turning the salmon to coat, until the sauce thickens and the edges are golden. Watch closely — the sauce should thicken to a glaze, not evaporate.
Serve bowls of coconut rice and broccoli topped with salmon, drizzled with the pan sauce.

title: Honey Roasted Chicken, Carrots, and Brussels

ingredients.item: carrots, cut into 2-inch pieces on the bias
garlic clove, minced (or 1/4 tsp garlic powder)

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F and line a baking sheet with parchment.
Whisk honey, oil, cumin, paprika, cayenne, garlic, and salt in a large bowl.
Add chicken, carrots, and brussels sprouts to the baking sheet. Pour marinade over and toss to coat. Spread into a single layer — chicken skin-side up, brussels cut-side down.
Roast on the centre rack for 40 minutes. Stir the veg halfway through if desired. If the chicken skin starts to burn, cover loosely with foil.

Meanwhile, whisk tahini and lemon juice until smooth — add a splash of hot water if needed to work out the lumps. Stir in Greek yogurt, garlic, and a big pinch of salt and pepper. Taste and adjust, adding more lemon for a tangier sauce.
Stir cilantro or parsley into the chicken and veg. Serve with tahini yogurt.

title: Jammy Tomato Feta Rice with Herby Pork Tenderloin
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: fennel bulb, stalks and root trimmed, thinly sliced
kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
dried green herbs (oregano, dill, basil, parsley, herbs de Provence, etc.)

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Add fennel, shallots, garlic, cherry tomatoes, feta block, ¼ cup olive oil, 1½ teaspoons salt, and several grinds of pepper to a 9x12-inch baking dish. Toss to coat.
Roast 45–55 minutes until tomatoes are collapsing and fennel and shallots are tender and beginning to brown.
Meanwhile, toss pork tenderloins with 2 tablespoons olive oil, dried herbs, Dijon, 2 teaspoons salt, and several grinds of pepper. Let marinate at room temperature.
Remove vegetables from oven and increase heat to 425°F. Bring 2½ cups water to a boil.

Sprinkle rice over the vegetables, then pour boiling water over top. Cover tightly with foil. Place pork tenderloins on top of the foil, about an inch apart. (Use a separate baking sheet if the foil is thin and might tear.)
Roast for 25 minutes total. Start checking the pork's internal temperature after 15 minutes — at 140°F, transfer to a cutting board and tent with foil to rest to 145°F.
After 25 minutes, remove the baking dish from the oven but leave the foil on for another 10 minutes to let the rice finish steaming.
Remove foil and stir the rice, breaking up tomatoes and feta. Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and acidity (a splash of lemon juice or vinegar if the tomatoes were sweet).
Slice pork and serve over the rice. Garnish with fresh herbs or drizzle with pesto.
notes: 90 min total, ~30 min active. Serves 4–6 — good weekend recipe.

title: Lemon Basil Pasta

ingredients.item: dried spaghetti or any long noodle
Parmigiano-Reggiano, freshly grated, plus more for garnish
lemon zest (about 1 tsp), plus more for garnish

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook pasta until al dente per package instructions. Before draining, reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water. Drain.
Meanwhile, whisk together olive oil, egg, Parmesan, lemon zest, lemon juice, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and pepper in a large serving bowl.
Thinly slice the basil.

Immediately add the hot drained pasta to the sauce along with 1/2 cup of the hot reserved pasta water and most of the basil. Toss continuously until the sauce is glossy and coats the noodles — it will look soupy at first, keep tossing. The cheese, oil, and egg need heat and time to come together. Add more pasta water if the sauce tightens up.
Serve immediately garnished with more Parmesan, lemon zest, and basil.
notes: Optional: lemon-parm crunchies topping — see source link for recipe.

title: Lemon Parm Chicken, Quinoa, and Kale

ingredients.item: roasted salted almonds, pepitas, or pistachios, chopped
fresh soft herbs — basil, parsley, or cilantro (optional)

steps: Make the dressing: combine all vinaigrette ingredients in a jar and shake until combined. Adjust seasoning to taste. Keeps refrigerated for up to 10 days.

Cook quinoa: bring quinoa and water to a boil in a small pot. Reduce to low, cover, and cook for 13–15 minutes until all liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat, keep covered for 5 more minutes, then fluff with a fork. You'll use 2 cups cooked quinoa for this recipe — refrigerate the rest for another use.
Add kale to a large bowl and season with a big pinch of salt and pepper. Massage with your hands about 10 times until it just starts to feel damp. Discard any tough stems.
Pull and shred the breast meat from the rotisserie chicken. Reserve legs, thighs, and wings in an airtight container in the fridge for another meal within 4 days.
Add the shredded chicken, 2 cups cooked quinoa, shredded carrots, Parmesan, and raisins to the kale. Add dressing to taste — about 1/3 cup if serving immediately, less if meal prepping. Toss to coat.
Divide into bowls and garnish with more Parmesan, chopped nuts, and a lemon wedge. Add nuts at serving rather than tossing in to keep them from going soggy.
notes: Serves 4–6. Consider doubling the quinoa and freezing the extra for future use.

title: Lemony Chicken & Orzo Soup
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: cannellini beans, drained but not rinsed
kosher salt and pepper (white pepper if available)
grated Parmesan, plus more for garnish
kale, Swiss chard, escarole, or spinach, chopped

steps: Dice 1 medium yellow onion and 3 large carrots.
Warm 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and carrot and cook for 4 minutes, stirring often.
Mince 4 garlic cloves. Add to pot and stir for 30 seconds.
Stir in 2 quarts chicken broth, 1½ pounds chicken thighs, 1 cup orzo, 1 can drained cannellini beans, ½ teaspoon kosher salt, and ¼ teaspoon pepper.
Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, whisk together 4 eggs, ⅓ cup lemon juice, ¼ cup grated Parmesan, and zest of 1 lemon until fully combined with no streaks of white. Set aside.
De-rib and chop 1 bunch kale. Set aside.
After 20 minutes, transfer chicken to a cutting board. Turn off the stove and move pot to a cool burner for 5 minutes. Shred and roughly chop the chicken; leave on the board.
Ladle ½ cup broth into the egg mixture, whisking as you pour. Ladle in 1 more cup and whisk to combine (this is tempering — it prevents scrambled egg soup).
Stir the tempered egg mixture into the pot. Add the chopped kale and chicken.
Cook over low heat, stirring often, until soup has thickened and kale is tender.
Taste and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed.
Serve garnished with 1 cup chopped soft green herbs divided between 6 bowls, extra Parmesan, and optional lemon slices. As the soup sits it will thicken — stir in broth or water to loosen.
notes: Serves 6 — leftover opportunity for a household of 3. Eggs and lemon juice make a silky broth (avgolemono-style).

title: No-Stir Mushroom Risotto

ingredients.item: beef stock (or water + beef-flavored Better Than Bouillon), divided
white wine (not sweet; or 1/4 cup red)
Parmigiano Reggiano, grated, plus more for garnish
raw walnuts (or pecans, hazelnuts, or any nut)

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F with a rack in the middle.

Warm olive oil in a large oven-proof pot over high heat. Add onions and mushrooms and toss to coat. Let sit for 3 minutes, stir, then let sit for another 3 minutes — high heat helps moisture evaporate and browns them quickly. Season with 1 teaspoon salt and 1/4 teaspoon pepper and continue stirring until very tender and browned.
Stir in the brown rice and cook for 1 minute to toast the grains. Pour in 6 cups stock and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot (a baking sheet works if there's no lid) and transfer to the oven. Bake for 50–60 minutes until all liquid is absorbed.

While the risotto bakes, make the Walnut Chili Crunch: warm olive oil in a medium skillet over medium heat. Crush walnuts in your fist as you add them. Toast with a pinch of salt for 6–8 minutes until golden. Add lemon zest and red pepper flakes and stir for 30 seconds — no longer or they'll burn. Turn off heat, stir in parsley, and season to taste.

Remove the pot from the oven and place over low heat. Stir in the white wine, remaining 1/4 cup stock, lemon juice, Gruyère, and Parmesan until the cheese is melted and the risotto is loose and saucy. Add more stock if needed — it should not be thick and gloopy.
Taste and adjust with more lemon, Parmesan, or salt as needed.
Serve in bowls topped with Parmesan and a generous sprinkle of Walnut Chili Crunch.

title: No-Stir Zucchini Risotto

ingredients.item: reduced-sodium chicken or vegetable stock (or 4 cups water + 4 tsp Better Than Bouillon)
Parmesan cheese, grated (ideally Parmigiano Reggiano), plus more for garnish

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F with a rack in the middle.
Bring stock to a boil in a large oven-proof heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over high heat. Meanwhile, mince shallots and garlic and grate zucchini.
Turn off the heat and stir in the arborio rice, grated zucchini, shallots, and garlic. Cover (a baking sheet works if the pot has no lid) and transfer to the oven. Bake for 40 minutes.

Remove from the oven and stir in Parmesan, butter, and the zest and juice of half the lemon. Season with salt and pepper. If too thick, add a splash of stock to loosen. If too thin, let sit a minute or two — the rice will keep absorbing liquid.
Serve as-is or topped with any protein. Garnish with extra Parmesan, lemon zest, and basil.
notes: Serves 6 — very filling as a vegetarian main.

title: One-Pan Caramelized Onion Pasta
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: Boursin Garlic and Herbs (5.2 oz package)
clear vinegar or lemon juice (e.g. champagne vinegar)
more grated Parm and red pepper flakes (optional, for serving)

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F.

In a 9x13-inch baking dish, layer in this order: spaghetti on the bottom, then onions, garlic, olive oil, tomato paste, Better Than Bouillon, anchovy paste, Italian seasoning, salt, and red pepper flakes. Use your hands to toss the onions with the pastes, oil, and seasonings — leave the spaghetti on the bottom.
Pour hot water in along one corner of the dish to avoid washing the seasoning off the onions. Place the Boursin block on top. Cover tightly with foil or a baking sheet.
Bake 45 minutes. Uncover (do not stir) and bake another 10–15 minutes until onions are browned and soft.
Add vinegar and Parm and toss with tongs until combined and saucy. (Discard any noodles that stayed hard.) Taste and adjust: salt, red pepper flakes, vinegar, or a splash of hot water for more sauce.
Serve topped with extra Parm and red pepper flakes. (If the pasta absorbs the sauce as it sits, add a splash of hot water and toss to loosen.)
notes: 80 min total but mostly hands-off. Serves 6–8 — flags as a leftover opportunity for a household of 3.

title: One-Pan Orecchiette Puttanesca
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: olive oil, plus 2 tablespoons for garnish
hot smoked paprika (or any smoked paprika)
pitted green olives (e.g. Nocellara or Castelvetrano), roughly chopped
caraway seeds, lightly toasted and crushed

steps: Add olive oil, garlic, chickpeas, smoked paprika, cumin, tomato paste, and ½ teaspoon salt to a large sauté pan over medium-high heat. Cook 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until chickpeas are slightly crisp. (Lower heat if they colour too quickly.) Remove ⅓ of the chickpeas and set aside for garnish.

While chickpeas cook, combine parsley, lemon zest, capers, and olives in a small bowl. If caraway seeds aren't pre-toasted, toast in a dry skillet over medium heat 5 minutes until fragrant, then crush with a rolling pin or mortar and pestle.
To the pan with the remaining chickpeas, add ⅔ of the parsley mixture, cherry tomatoes, sugar, and crushed caraway seeds. Cook 2 minutes over medium-high, stirring often.

Add orecchiette, stock, ¾ teaspoon salt, and water. Bring to a simmer, reduce heat to medium, cover, and cook 12–14 minutes until pasta is al dente. (If liquid is gone but pasta isn't done, add a splash more water and cook covered another 1–2 minutes. If liquid remains but pasta is ready, remove lid, crank heat to high, and stir constantly until liquid evaporates.)
Stir in remaining parsley mixture. Drizzle with the extra 2 tablespoons olive oil and top with reserved crispy chickpeas and a grind of black pepper.
notes: One-pan pasta — everything cooks together. Serves 4.

title: One-Pot Cheesy Pumpkin Pasta

ingredients.item: canned pumpkin or butternut squash purée

steps: Preheat oven to broil on high.
In a large ovenproof pot (such as a Dutch oven), combine pasta, milk, water, salt, garlic powder, thyme, smoked paprika, mustard powder, nutmeg, and pepper.
Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat, then reduce to medium. Cook 8–10 minutes until pasta is tender, stirring often to prevent milk from scorching and pasta from sticking.

When milk has reduced to the consistency of cream, turn off heat and let cool a couple minutes. Stir in pumpkin purée. Add all the Gouda and all but ½ cup of the Cheddar one handful at a time, stirring to melt. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Sprinkle breadcrumbs and remaining ½ cup Cheddar over the top.
Broil 2–5 minutes until golden brown and cheese has melted. (Watch closely — broilers vary.)

title: One-Pot Lemony Garlic Butter Shrimp Orzo

ingredients.item: red pepper flakes (omit or reduce for spice-averse)
low-sodium chicken stock, plus more as needed
Parmesan cheese, grated, plus more for garnish
fresh basil, finely chopped, plus more for garnish

steps: Combine shrimp, half the grated garlic, all the lemon zest, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and 3/4 teaspoon salt in a bowl. Set aside to marinate at room temperature.

Melt butter in a 12-inch skillet or braiser over medium heat. Cook the shallot, remaining garlic, and red pepper flakes until softened, about 4 minutes. Stir in 3 cups chicken stock and orzo and bring to a boil. Cover, reduce to low, and cook for 8 minutes.

Remove the lid and lay the marinated shrimp in an even layer over the orzo. Cover and cook for 5 minutes, until most shrimp look cooked through (some translucency is fine — they'll finish in the next step).

Stir in the frozen broccoli, Parmesan, lemon juice, and basil. Add more stock if the skillet looks dry. Stir until all shrimp are opaque and curled into a C-shape and the orzo is a loose, risotto-like consistency. Add stock a splash at a time to loosen if needed.
Serve garnished with extra basil, red pepper flakes, and Parmesan.
notes: Peas substituted with frozen broccoli.

title: One-Pot Spaghetti al Limone
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps: Add spaghetti to a large wide saucepan. Grate in the lemon zest and garlic. Add butter, 1 teaspoon salt, and several grinds of pepper. Pour in water.

Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-high. Use tongs to turn the pasta every 30 seconds until al dente and most (but not all) of the water has evaporated, 7–9 minutes. (If water evaporates before pasta is done, add a splash more.)
Stir in ¼ cup lemon juice. Remove from heat and let cool for 3 minutes. (Do not skip — the cheese will clump if added while too hot.)

A little at a time, add the finely grated Pecorino, tossing vigorously with tongs after each addition before adding more. Keep stirring until saucy and combined. (For a creamier result, add more butter or a splash of water.)
Taste and adjust with more lemon juice, salt, or cheese.
Serve immediately, topped with extra Pecorino and black pepper.
notes: One-pot, 20-minute pasta. Everything cooks together in a single pot — minimal cleanup. Serves 4 (possibly up to 6).

title: Rotisserie-ish Chicken with Schmaltzy Cabbage

ingredients.item: green cabbage head (~2 lbs), cored and thinly sliced
apple cider vinegar or lemon juice

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Core and thinly slice the cabbage into 1/4-inch pieces. Toss on your largest rimmed baking sheet with 3 tablespoons olive oil and 1 1/2 teaspoons salt.
Stir together 2 tablespoons olive oil, 1 tablespoon salt, brown sugar, paprika, chili powder, and pepper in a small bowl.

Pat the chicken completely dry. Place breast-side up and coat all over with the spice paste. Tuck the wings under the bird to prevent burning. Tie the legs together with kitchen twine (or a rolled strip of foil).
Prick the sweet potatoes all over with a fork and rub with a thin coat of olive oil. Place on the cabbage or on a separate sheet pan above the chicken.

Roast on the centre rack for 45–60 minutes, stirring the cabbage every 20 minutes, until a thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the leg reads 160°F. If the skin darkens too early, cover the chicken loosely with foil. Start checking at 45 minutes.
Remove from the oven. Tilt the chicken over the sheet pan using tongs through the cavity to drain the internal juices over the cabbage. Transfer the chicken to a cutting board to rest.
Return cabbage and sweet potatoes to the oven for 10 minutes until the cabbage edges are brown and frizzled. Broil for a minute if needed, watching closely.
Stir vinegar into the cabbage and season to taste. Halve the sweet potatoes and mash 1 tablespoon butter and a pinch of salt and pepper into each.
Carve the chicken and serve with the cabbage and a sweet potato.
notes: Whole chicken time varies by bird size and oven — start checking at 45 minutes.

title: Saucy Chicken and Cabbage Satay Bowls
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: sesame oil or neutral cooking oil (e.g. olive oil), divided
fresh ginger, grated or minced (or ¼ tsp ground ginger)
garlic cloves, grated or minced (or ¼ tsp garlic powder)
rice vinegar or juice of ½ lime

steps: In your largest skillet, warm 1 tablespoon oil over medium-high heat. Add coleslaw mix, mushrooms, and scallions. Cook 3–5 minutes until just beginning to soften. Push to one side.

Warm remaining 1 tablespoon oil in the empty half. Add ground chicken and cook undisturbed 2–3 minutes, then break into small pieces and cook through, 8–10 minutes total. Stir the cabbage occasionally as the chicken cooks.
Once chicken is cooked through, stir everything together. If liquid remains, increase heat to high and stir until evaporated.

Reduce to medium-high. Season with a big pinch of salt, then stir in coconut milk, cilantro, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, peanut butter, fish sauce, sriracha, rice vinegar, honey, curry powder, and cumin.
Cook 3–5 minutes until sauce thickens and clings to the chicken and vegetables. (If still liquidy after 5 minutes, increase to high heat — it will thicken quickly.)
Taste and adjust: salt to pop the flavors, lime/vinegar for tang, honey for sweetness, sriracha for heat.
Serve over rice or on its own. Garnish with sriracha, chopped peanuts, and sesame seeds.
notes: Avocado oil subbed with olive oil. Serves 4 with rice, 3 without. Quick weeknight bowl.

title: Saucy Ground Beef Teriyaki Bowls

ingredients.item: low-sodium soy sauce or coconut aminos
jasmine rice, cooked per package instructions

steps: Cook rice per package instructions. Remove from heat and keep covered.
Make the teriyaki sauce: whisk soy sauce and cornstarch together until fully dissolved. Add water, sesame oil, coconut sugar, garlic powder, and ginger and whisk until combined. Set aside.
Prep all veg and keep them separate — they go in at different times.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and sauté for 4 minutes until soft. Add garlic and cook for 1 more minute. Add ground beef and cook for about 4 minutes, breaking into small pieces.
Add bell pepper, broccoli, and carrots. Cook until beef is cooked through and veg has softened.
Pour teriyaki sauce over and stir for a few minutes until thickened.
Fluff rice and assemble bowls. Spoon extra sauce over the top and garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
notes: Avocado oil substituted with olive oil.

title: Sheet Pan Gnocchi with Sausage and Brussels

ingredients.item: extra-virgin olive oil, plus more for drizzling
mild or spicy Italian sausage links

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Trim any brown roots from the brussels sprouts. Cut through the root end into thin planks — about 5–6 per sprout.

Place brussels sprouts, mushrooms, and gnocchi (straight from the package, no pre-cooking needed) on your largest rimmed baking sheet. Grate the garlic and lemon zest directly over the top. Add olive oil, salt, and pepper and toss to coat. Spread into an even layer.

Squeeze the sausage out of the casing in 1/2-inch increments directly onto the sheet pan to create small meatballs — about 5 per link. They don't need to be perfectly round. Spread everything out as much as possible.
Take the burrata out of the fridge to come to room temperature.
Roast for 25–30 minutes until crispy on the outside and tender inside. Broil for a minute at the end if the top needs more colour — watch closely.
Divide between four bowls. Top each with half a ball of burrata, a drizzle of olive oil, and red pepper flakes or pesto if using.

title: Sheet Pan Greek Chicken and Potatoes

ingredients.item: fingerling potatoes, halved (or quartered if large)

steps: Whisk 2 tablespoons olive oil, zest and juice of half the lemon, grated garlic, 1 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, and oregano in a large bowl. Add chicken thighs and toss to coat. Marinate in the refrigerator for up to 12 hours, or proceed immediately.
Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a large rimmed baking sheet with parchment.
Toss potatoes and fennel with remaining 2 tablespoons olive oil, 3/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper on the baking sheet. Spread into an even layer. Roast for 15 minutes, then stir.
Shake excess marinade off the chicken. Nestle skin-side up between the potatoes and fennel. Pat the skin dry with a paper towel to ensure crispiness.
Roast for 30–35 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F and potatoes and fennel are browned and tender.
Finish with juice of the remaining lemon half, crumbled feta, and fresh dill. Season to taste and serve immediately.
notes: Fennel: see source link for prep notes. Can substitute with thinly sliced onion or leek if unavailable.

title: Sorta Pasta alla Norma
steps: Peel eggplant in stripes ("like a zebra"), then cut into ½-inch cubes. Warm ¼ cup olive oil in a wide skillet over medium-high heat. Add eggplant and 1 teaspoon salt and toss to coat. Cook, stirring every minute, 8–10 minutes until very tender and browned. Transfer to a bowl.
Return skillet to medium-high, warm remaining 1 tablespoon oil. Add sausage and cook, breaking into small crumbles, until almost cooked through. Add garlic and continue cooking until sausage is done.
Add cooked eggplant and pearl couscous. Stir for a few minutes to lightly toast the couscous. Stir in marinara sauce, water, and ¼ teaspoon salt.
Once the liquid reaches a boil, reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook 15–18 minutes until pasta is tender.
Stir in ¼ cup Parmesan and most of the basil (reserve some for garnish).
Serve topped with remaining Parmesan, basil, and red pepper flakes.

title: Spicy (or Not) Salmon Tempura Crunch Bowls

ingredients.item: sriracha (1 tbsp for flavor, 2 for heat)
English cucumber, halved and thinly sliced into half moons

ingredients.amount: thinly sliced apple, fennel, red onion, scallions, or peanuts

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add brown rice and boil like pasta for about 40 minutes until tender. Drain, return to pot, stir in butter and a big pinch of salt. Cover until serving.
Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment.
Season salmon with salt, garlic powder, and ginger powder. Rub with soy sauce, sriracha, 1 tablespoon sesame oil, and maple syrup. Place skin-side down on the baking sheet.

Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a bowl. Stir in remaining 1 tablespoon sesame oil, panko, sesame seeds, and a pinch of salt. Chop the entire bunch of cilantro. Finely dice 1/3 cup and stir into the panko; set the rest aside for the salad. Spread the breadcrumb mixture over the salmon.
Bake for 15–18 minutes until the salmon flakes easily when prodded with a fork.
Combine mixed greens, remaining cilantro, and cucumber in a large bowl. Dress with lime juice, sesame oil, and a big pinch of salt. Toss and adjust seasoning to taste.
Make the sriracha mayo: stir together mayo, sriracha, lime juice, and a pinch of salt. Adjust heat to taste.
Serve bowl-style with buttery rice on one side, dressed greens on the other, and salmon on top, drizzled with sriracha mayo. Add optional toppings as desired.

title: Turkey Zucchini Burgers

ingredients.item: lettuce, tomato, onion or other toppings

steps: Grate 2 cups of zucchini onto a thin dish towel. Squeeze out as much moisture as possible over a sink. Transfer to a large bowl. Slice any remaining zucchini into planks to grill alongside the burgers.

Add ground turkey, lemon zest, breadcrumbs, basil or mint, egg yolk, salt, garlic powder, and pepper to the bowl. Mix with hands until combined. Divide into 4 patties slightly larger than an English muffin.
Place patties on a parchment-lined baking sheet and freeze for 20 minutes (or refrigerate up to 48 hours). Freezing is essential for grilling — skip only if cooking in a skillet.
Make the miso mayo: stir together mayo, miso, and 1 tablespoon lemon juice until smooth. Can be made up to 1 week ahead.
Just before serving, toss together watermelon, feta, basil or mint, and vinegar or lemon juice for the salad.
Preheat outdoor grill to 500°F. Spray patties all over with cooking spray — this is essential for preventing sticking. Grill for 4 minutes per side, or until they reach 165°F internal temperature.
After flipping the burgers, spray the cut sides of the English muffins with cooking spray and grill cut-side down for about 1 minute until toasted.
Slather muffins with miso mayo, top with burgers and toppings of choice. Serve alongside the watermelon feta salad.
notes: Substitute: sub grilled zucchini planks (from the leftover zucchini) for a side instead of the watermelon salad if preferred.

title: Winter Stew
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: large carrots, washed and chopped into bite-sized pieces
garlic cloves, thinly sliced or minced
small Yukon gold potato (4–6 oz), chopped into bite-sized pieces
low-sodium chicken stock (or water + Better Than Bouillon)

steps: Warm olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add carrots, onion, and a big pinch of salt. Cook 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Add garlic, ground turkey, and another big pinch of salt. Cook, breaking turkey into small pieces, until cooked through.

Add potato, stock, farro, lentils, crushed tomatoes, the Parm rind, and harissa (start with 1 tablespoon; add more later for heat). Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook uncovered 20 minutes until farro is tender.
Meanwhile, roughly chop kale and zest and juice the lemon.
Remove and discard the Parm rind. Stir in kale, lemon zest, lemon juice, and cream cheese (if using). Stir until kale is wilted and cream cheese has melted in.
Adjust consistency: stir in more stock to thin, or keep simmering uncovered to thicken.
Serve topped with plenty of freshly grated Parm and a dollop of harissa.
notes: Serves 6 — leftover opportunity for a household of 3. Cream cheese is optional but makes it creamy.

title: 10-Minute Pantry Pasta Salad

ingredients.item: 12 ounces red lentil or brown rice pasta, cooked according to package instructions and cooled (I love the Trader Joe’s red lentil penne)
1 cup roasted red peppers, thinly sliced

steps: Add the cooked and cooled pasta to a large mixing bowl. Add the chickpeas, roasted red peppers, feta, olives, parsley, and green onions. Toss to evenly distribute.
Add the olive oil, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, oregano, salt, garlic powder, and black pepper. Toss again to coat all ingredients in the dressing.
Chill in the refrigerator until serving.
notes: "> Notes For some extra protein you could add some salami, chicken, shrimp, or steak! Store leftovers in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 4 days. Serve chilled. {"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org","@type":"Recipe","name":"10-MINUTE PANTRY PASTA SALAD","image":["https:\/\/jenneatsgoood.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2024\/07\/IMG_3479_Original-e1719928131593.jpg","https:\/\/i0.wp.com\/jenneatsgoood.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2024\/07\/IMG_3479_Original-e1719928131593.jpg?resize=500%2C500

title: 20-Minute Summer Sauté
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: zucchini, sliced into thin half circles
chicken sausages (Italian, roasted garlic, or chicken & apple), sliced into ¼-inch rounds
feta and fresh soft green herbs (optional, for topping)

steps: Cut zucchini in half vertically, then into thin half-circle slices.

Warm 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat in your largest skillet. Add zucchini in an even layer. Cook 4–5 minutes until golden-brown, stir, cook the other side another 3–4 minutes. Season with ½ teaspoon kosher salt.
While zucchini cooks, slice chicken sausages and halve cherry tomatoes.
Push zucchini to one side of the skillet off the flame. Add a drizzle of oil to the empty side, then add sausage and tomatoes. Brown all over, 4–5 minutes.
Mince garlic while sausage and tomatoes cook.
Add a little more oil if dry, then add garlic, corn, and several grinds of black pepper. Stir until corn is hot and excess moisture has evaporated, about 2 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar. Taste — add up to 1 more tablespoon for extra tanginess. Adjust salt as needed.
Divide between two plates or bowls. Garnish with feta and fresh herbs if desired.
notes: Serves 2 — doesn't feed a household of 3 without scaling. Flag when planning.

title: 20-Minute Veggie Curry

ingredients.item: 1 cup dry rice, cooked for serving

steps: Begin by heating the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced red peppers and mushrooms and sauté, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes to soften.

Add the garlic and all ground spices and mix to combine. Continuously stir for 2 minutes until the spices are aromatic. Pour in the tomato puree and veggie broth, stir, and bring everything to a simmer.

Add the chickpeas to the skillet and incorporate. Pour in the coconut milk. Stir and let everything simmer for at least 5 minutes to allow the flavors to combine (if you want to let it simmer longer, reduce heat to low and stir every 5-10 minutes until serving).
Serve the curry in even servings on top of the cooked rice and enjoy immediately for best results.

title: 30-Minute Cheesy Broccoli Pesto Pasta with Lemon Pepper Chicken
source: What to Cook When You Don't Feel Like Cooking

ingredients.item: broccoli florets (or one 10 oz steam-in-bag bag)
grated Parmigiano-Reggiano, plus more for serving
packed basil leaves, plus more for serving

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook pasta about 1 minute less than package instructions. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining. Drain and set aside.
Steam broccoli florets until bright green and crisp-tender, about 5 minutes (steamer basket over pasta water, boiled in the same pot after the pasta, or microwaved in a steam-in-bag).
Pat chicken thighs dry, drizzle with EVOO, season both sides generously with salt and lots of pepper. Zest 1 lemon evenly over the chicken.

Heat a large cast-iron or non-stick skillet over medium-high heat (no oil needed). Add chicken thighs and cook 4 minutes. Flip when golden brown. Cook another 4–5 minutes until cooked through (165°F). Squeeze the zested lemon over the cooked chicken and let rest.
Blend steamed broccoli, cottage cheese, Parm, garlic, basil, spinach, milk, a large pinch of salt, and the zest and juice of the second lemon until smooth. Add more milk if too thick.
Return pasta pot to medium heat. Add broccoli pesto and ¼ cup pasta water. Toss to coat until warm and glossy, about 2 minutes. Add more pasta water to loosen if needed. Season to taste.
Serve pasta topped with sliced chicken, freshly grated Parm, basil leaves, a drizzle of EVOO, a squeeze of lemon, and flaky salt.
notes: Broccoli pesto sauce doubles as a great sauce for other dishes. Serves 4–6.

title: Apple, Cheddar, and Date Chopped Salad with Chicken

ingredients.item: 1 small cabbage head, finely chopped (about 4 cups)
4 ounces uncured bacon, cooked and finely chopped (optional)
4 medjool dates, pitted and finely chopped

steps: Make the smoky maple dressing: In a bowl or jar, whisk or shake the olive oil, apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder until emulsified, about 45 seconds.

Preheat the oven to 400ºF and line a large sheet pan with parchment paper (if needed). Add the chicken breast to the sheet pan and drizzle olive oil on each side along with a generous pinch of salt and black pepper. Bake until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165ºF, about 25 minutes. Dice into 1/2-inch pieces.
Assemble the salad: Add the cabbage, kale, celery, apple to a large mixing bowl and toss with the vinaigrette. Add the chicken, bacon, cheddar, and dates and toss again to mix.
Let the salad sit on the counter for 10 to 15 minutes after mixing to allow the kale to break down slightly.
Serve between 6 bowls or store in a sealed container.
notes: : Apples: Apples are one of my favorite ways to spruce up a salad, add a bit of crunch, and complement a smoky dressing. We all know the saying, “an apple a day keeps the doctor away”, but with fiber, antioxidants, and vitamin C, it’s really true! You can also swap for grapes. Cheddar: I like to use a sharp cheese like cheddar in my salads to balance out the sweetness of the fruit, and provide a creamy texture in each bite. You can also swap for your favorite cheese like feta,

title: Autumn Chorizo Sheet PAN Meal

ingredients.item: 4 links of pork or chicken chorizo sausages

steps: Preheat oven to 375 degrees and line a sheet pan with parchment paper.
Chop the potato into small wedges, lay them flat on the pan, and drizzle with oil. Bake for 20 minutes, until tender to a fork.
While the potatoes cook, chop the apples into cubes and slice the brussel sprouts and chorizo. Add everything to a large bowl and mix in the olive oil and all spices.
Remove the potatoes from the oven and add the chorizo mix to the pan. Evenly distribute everything and try not to layer anything to allow the ingredients to get crisp.
Put the pan back in the oven and bake for another 20 minutes, until the apples are soft and the brussel sprouts are starting to brown.
Serve immediately for best results or store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to four days.

title: Autumn Veggie Stir Fry

ingredients.item: 2 tablespoons avocado or olive oil, divided
2 cups cubed butternut squash (use frozen cubed to save time)
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, plus additional to taste
1/4 cup coconut aminos, tamari, or reduced sodium gluten-free soy sauce

ingredients.amount: chopped parsley, green onion, or fried egg for a little something extra

steps: Bring 2 1/2 cups of salted water to a boil, then add the rinsed rice. Reduce heat to medium low and cover the pot. Cook until the rice has absorbed the water and is fluffy, 18 to 22 minutes.

While the rice cooks, heat 1 tablespoon of the oil over medium heat in a large skillet, season the chicken with a few pinches of salt, brown on each side and fully cook through, 5 to 7 minutes of each side. Remove from the skillet and cut into small pieces.

Add the remaining oil to the skillet, followed by the butternut squash, broccoli, and brussels sprouts. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the squash is tender, 6 to 10 minutes. Add the spinach and continue cooking until it is wilted down.

Add the rice to the skillet and season everything with the garlic powder, thyme, rosemary, and salt and stir to coat. Cook until fragrant, 2 to 3 minutes, then pour in the coconut aminos and maple syrup. Continue cooking for another 2 to 3 minutes to let the flavors combine.
Stir in the cashews, add back the chicken, and remove from heat. Serve immediately for best results.
notes: : Butternut Squash: This naturally sweet, starchy vegetable adds heartiness and a beautiful color to the stir fry. When sautéed, it softens and caramelizes, enhancing the overall comfort-food feel of the dish. Rich in vitamins A and C, it’s also a great source of fiber. Shaved Brussels Sprouts: Shaved brussels sprouts cook quickly and bring a slightly nutty, earthy flavor to the stir fry. Their texture softens just enough while still adding bite, making them a satisfying addition. They’re

title: Baked Black Bean & Sweet Potato Tacos with Chipotle Sauce

ingredients.item: 4-5 medium sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/2” cubes
15 oz can black beans, drained and rinsed
10-12 corn tortillas, plus olive oil and kosher salt to coat
1/2 cup fresh chopped cilantro (about 1/3 of a bunch)
3/4 cup full-fat coconut milk (half of a 13.5oz can)
4-6 chipotle peppers in adobo sauce (depending on preference)
1/2 tsp kosher salt, additional to taste

steps: Preheat oven to 400F and line a large sheet pan with parchment paper.

Toss the cubed sweet potatoes in the olive oil, salt, garlic powder, cumin, chili powder, and oregano. Transfer to the sheet pan and bake for 40-50 minutes, tossing halfway through, until fork tender with crispy edges.

While the sweet potatoes cook, make the chipotle sauce by blending the coconut milk, chipotle peppers, salt, and lime juice in a blender or food processor on high speed until completely smooth. Set aside.

To prepare the tortillas, lightly drizzle each with olive oil, and use clean hands to cover the entire surface with oil. Stack 2-3 tortillas in a lightly damp paper towel and microwave for about 20 seconds, repeat the process for all 12 tortillas.

Transfer the softened tortillas to a separate, unlined, large sheet pan. Add about 1 tbsp of the chipotle sauce to the center of each tortilla. Place even servings of the cooked sweet potatoes and black beans on one half of the tortilla (don't over-stuff) then fold the tortilla closed. (If they're difficult to fold, they're likely over-stuffed.)

Reduce the oven to 375F and bake the tacos until crispy, 12-16 minutes. Remove from the oven and immediately sprinkle salt over the tacos. To serve, top each set of tacos with chopped cilantro and a side of chipotle sauce for dipping.
notes: "> Your email address will not be published.
